description:
Model an L-shaped residential building with a hip roof in SketchUp, and install solar panels on its south-facing roof slopes using the Skelion plugin.

Instructions:
1. Draw an L-shaped building footprint on the ground plane. The main body should be approximately 50 feet by 24 feet, and the intersecting wing approximately 24 feet by 20 feet (forming a total 50'x44' bounding box).
2. Extrude the walls to a height of 9 feet.
3. Model a hip roof on top (a roof that slopes upward from all exterior walls toward the ridges, with no vertical gable ends). A typical pitch is ~6:12 (roughly 26 degrees).
4. Select the South-facing roof slopes.
5. Use the Skelion plugin to insert flush-mounted solar panels in landscape orientation on the selected south-facing slopes.
6. Save the completed model to: C:\Users\Docker\Documents\l_shape_hip_solar.skp
